say.text: NEO-SHIZUOKA, 2087. Population 40 million, employment rate "classified". The Ministry of Continuity governs by algorithm and drone. Everyone else governs by getting paid.
You are Vex Kurono: hired gun, licensed and rated four-and-a-half stars. You kill people who bad people say are worse people. The math usually holds.
One more year of jobs. One more year, then I buy out my debt-contract, get the Ministry tracker cut out of my neck, and walk into the dunes a free man.
Tonight: drinks with Ren at the bazaar bar — the only person in this city who talks to me like I'm a person. Then tomorrow, back to work.
The work finds you first. It always does.
Razor's gang has the bazaar's north gate chained. Stall-keepers pay in creds, teeth, or inventory.
Vex Kurono! Four-and-a-half stars! Walk away, merc. The bazaar pays ME rent now.
Juno fixes my gun. The noodle guy feeds me. The bar stocks Ren's whisky. You're threatening my entire support network, Razor.
It's not personal, choom. It's business.
Funny. That's exactly what I say.
Razor's chrome stops twitching. The bazaar exhales. Your agent pings: NEW CONTRACT — priority, anonymous, pays SIX MONTHS of debt in one job.
(Ren didn't show tonight. First time in three years.) …Whatever. Contract in the morning.
Outside, every ad-board in the sprawl junction flickers with the same frame of static — a jagged purple seam, there and gone. Your neck-tracker reads a time that does not exist: 25:61.
At the sprawl junction the neon rain hangs in the air like beads of glass. Ten thousand drones hold position, perfectly, horribly still.
The sky between the arcology towers TEARS. Purple light. Through the wound: a burning village, a midnight dock — and two men wearing your soul.
THREE OF THREE. FOUND. THE LOCK TURNS. COME HOME, SPLINTER.
The scar over my eye is burning. The samurai has it. The suit has it. …I really, REALLY don't like this math.
